core_mechanism: Distributed computation of a linear function over a finite field via local linear precoding (linear network coding): each source applies a local matrix map to its private symbols so that a single additive aggregation at the sink reconstructs a prescribed linear combination, with a shared correlated resource among the sources lowering total communication cost and making it subadditive across function instances
brief_reason: The method has non-communicating senders encode their finite-field data vectors W_i through judicious local matrix precoding and use shared entanglement so the receiver recovers the target linear combination Y = V_1 W_1 + ... + V_k W_k from a single aggregate measurement at reduced, subadditive communication cost.
